Label: Moderate Institutional Trust
Description: This segment shows a preference for moderate to high trust (scores 4-7) across most institutions, with the strongest confidence in the president and armed forces. Trust is notably lower for political parties and the national police.

Label: Very High Institutional Trust
Description: This segment is characterized by very high trust in the president (82%), public ministry (74%), and armed forces (72%), with a general tendency to select 'Mucho' across nearly all national institutions.

Label: High Institutional Trust
Description: This segment expresses high levels of trust across all measured institutions, with the strongest probabilities concentrated on scores of 5, 6, or 7, particularly for the president, armed forces, and the supreme court.

Label: Generalized Institutional Distrust
Description: This segment is characterized by very high probabilities of 'Nada' (not at all) across almost all institutions, most notably political parties (0.85) and the national police (0.76). The only notable exceptions are the president and armed forces, which show more distributed responses and higher probabilities of trust compared to other institutions.

Label: Presidential and Military Trust
Description: This segment is characterized by high trust in the president and armed forces, contrasted with very low trust in political parties and the national police.
